title: Intuitive Surgical Inc. (ISRG) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Intuitive Surgical Inc. (ISRG). Economic Prospect Score: 78/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Intuitive Surgical Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 15% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 10.0% WACC balances Intuitive Surgical Inc.'s earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.5% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisTitle: Intuitive Surgical Inc.: A Fundamental Assessment
thesisParagraphs: Intuitive Surgical Inc. (ISRG) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 15% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 10.0% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Intuitive Surgical Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 15% FCF growth and 10.0% WACC, Opus calculates ISRG's intrinsic value at approximately $264.07 per share, suggesting the stock is overvalued at $545.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive ISRG's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (15%) and discount rate (10.0%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.

prospectVerdictDetail: Monopoly in robotic surgery with 90%+ market share in soft tissue robotic procedures. The installed base of da Vinci systems creates a razor/blade model with recurring instrument revenue. Ion platform expands into lung biopsy.
prospectKeyRisks: Medtronic Hugo and J&J Ottava robotic surgery platforms gaining regulatory clearances and offering lower-cost alternatives
Hospital capital budgets tightening in a recession, delaying new system purchases
Da Vinci 5 transition creating short-term revenue headwinds as customers pause orders to wait for the new platform
prospectKeyCatalysts: Da Vinci 5 driving system upgrades and expanding the addressable procedure set beyond current 7M annual procedures
Ion bronchoscopy platform reaching inflection point for lung cancer screening, addressing a $5B+ TAM
Procedure growth in emerging markets (China, India) where robotic surgery penetration is under 5%
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
